# Graph Memory — Full Documentation

> MCP server that turns a project directory into a queryable semantic knowledge base with 70 tools, REST API, and web UI.

Graph Memory indexes markdown documentation, TypeScript/JavaScript source code, and all project files into six interconnected graph structures. It runs locally with no external API calls — embeddings are computed on-device using ONNX models.

---

## Installation

### npm (recommended)

```bash
npm install -g @graphmemory/server
```

Requires Node.js >= 22. The default embedding model (`Xenova/jina-embeddings-v2-small-en`, ~33 MB) downloads on first startup and is cached at `~/.graph-memory/models/`.

### npx (no install)

```bash
npx @graphmemory/server serve
```

### Docker

```bash
docker run -d \
  --name graph-memory \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v $(pwd):/data/projects/my-project:ro \
  -v graph-memory-models:/data/models \
  ghcr.io/graph-memory/graphmemory-server
```

Multi-platform images (amd64 + arm64) on GitHub Container Registry.

### From source

```bash
git clone https://github.com/graph-memory/graphmemory.git
cd graphmemory && npm install && npm run build
node dist/cli/index.js serve
```

---

## Quick Start

```bash
cd /path/to/your-project
graphmemory serve
```

No config file needed — the current directory becomes your project. Server starts at http://localhost:3000 with the web UI.

### Connect an AI assistant

**Claude Code:**
```bash
claude mcp add --transport http --scope project graph-memory http://localhost:3000/mcp/your-project
```

**Claude Desktop:** Settings > Connectors > add URL: `http://localhost:3000/mcp/your-project`

**Cursor / Windsurf** (`.mcp.json`):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "graph-memory": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "http://localhost:3000/mcp/your-project"
    }
  }
}
```

The project ID is your directory name (e.g., `my-app` for `/path/to/my-app`).

---

## Configuration

All configuration is in `graph-memory.yaml`. Running without a config file uses the current directory as a single project with all defaults.

### Minimal multi-project config

```yaml
projects:
  my-app:
    projectDir: "/path/to/my-app"
  docs-site:
    projectDir: "/path/to/docs-site"

server:
  port: 3000
```

### Key settings

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `projectDir` | (required) | Absolute path to project directory |
| `include` | `["**/*.md"]` | Glob patterns for doc indexing |
| `code` | `["**/*.ts", "**/*.js", ...]` | Glob patterns for code indexing |
| `exclude` | `["node_modules/**", ...]` | Glob patterns to exclude |
| `model` | `"Xenova/jina-embeddings-v2-small-en"` | Embedding model for docs/knowledge/tasks/skills |
| `codeModel` | (inherits `model`) | Separate embedding model for code graph |
| `server.port` | `3000` | HTTP port |
| `server.host` | `"127.0.0.1"` | Bind address |
| `server.modelsDir` | `"~/.graph-memory/models"` | Model cache directory |
| `server.sessionTimeout` | `3600` | MCP session timeout in seconds |
| `server.defaultAccess` | `"readwrite"` | Default access level |

### Embedding model configuration

```yaml
server:
  embedding:
    model: "Xenova/jina-embeddings-v2-small-en"
    pooling: "mean"
    normalize: true
    dtype: "q8"
    docPrefix: ""
```

Per-graph model override:

```yaml
projects:
  my-app:
    projectDir: "/path/to/my-app"
    model: "Xenova/jina-embeddings-v2-small-en"
    codeModel: "jinaai/jina-embeddings-v2-base-code"
```

### Remote embedding

Delegate embedding to a GPU server:

```yaml
server:
  embedding:
    model: "http://gpu-server:8080/embed"
```

The remote endpoint receives `POST { texts: string[] }` and returns `{ embeddings: number[][] }`.

### Redis (optional)

```yaml
server:
  redis:
    enabled: true
    url: "redis://localhost:6379"
    prefix: "gm:"
    embeddingCacheTtl: 86400
```

Redis is used for session store and embedding cache. When disabled (default), in-memory fallback is used.

### Authentication

```yaml
server:
  auth:
    users:
      - username: admin
        password: "$scrypt$..."
    apiKeys:
      - name: ci-bot
        key: "gm_..."
        access: readonly

  oauth:
    enabled: true
    accessTokenTtl: 3600
    refreshTokenTtl: 604800
    authCodeTtl: 600
```

Add users interactively: `graphmemory users add --config graph-memory.yaml`

### Access control

5-level ACL resolution chain: graph → project → workspace → server.access → server.defaultAccess.

```yaml
projects:
  my-app:
    projectDir: "/path/to/my-app"
    access: readwrite
    graphs:
      docs:
        access: readonly
      knowledge:
        access: readwrite
```

Access levels: `readwrite`, `readonly`, `none`.

### Workspaces

Share knowledge, tasks, and skills across related projects:

```yaml
workspaces:
  platform:
    projects: [api, web, worker]
    access: readwrite

projects:
  api:
    projectDir: "/path/to/api"
  web:
    projectDir: "/path/to/web"
  worker:
    projectDir: "/path/to/worker"
```

Shared graphs: KnowledgeGraph, TaskGraph, SkillGraph. Per-project graphs: DocGraph, CodeGraph, FileIndexGraph.

---

## CLI Reference

### `graphmemory serve`

Start HTTP server with MCP, REST API, and web UI.

```bash
graphmemory serve [options]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--config <path>` | Path to `graph-memory.yaml` |
| `--project <id>` | Serve a single project |
| `--port <number>` | Override server port |
| `--host <address>` | Override bind address |
| `--reindex` | Force re-index all projects on startup |
| `--log-level` | Log level: fatal/error/warn/info/debug/trace |

### `graphmemory index`

One-shot index: scan, embed, save, and exit.

```bash
graphmemory index --config graph-memory.yaml --project my-app
```

### `graphmemory users add`

Add a user to the config file interactively (prompts for username and password).

```bash
graphmemory users add --config graph-memory.yaml
```

---

## Graphs

### DocGraph
Indexes markdown files into heading-based chunks. Each chunk is a node with an embedding. Edges represent document structure (parent/child headings) and cross-file links.

### CodeGraph
Parses TypeScript/JavaScript via tree-sitter AST. Extracts functions, classes, interfaces, types, enums, and their relationships (calls, imports, extends, implements).

### KnowledgeGraph
User-managed notes and facts with typed relations. Supports file attachments, cross-graph links (to docs, code, files, tasks, skills), and file mirror to `.notes/` directory.

### FileIndexGraph
Indexes all project files with directory hierarchy, language detection, and MIME types. Provides file-level search without reading content.

### TaskGraph
Kanban-style task management with statuses: `backlog`, `todo`, `in_progress`, `review`, `done`, `cancelled`. Supports priorities, due dates, estimates, assignees, tags, cross-graph links, and file mirror to `.tasks/` directory.

### SkillGraph
Reusable procedures/recipes with steps, triggers, tags, and usage tracking. Cross-graph links and file mirror to `.skills/` directory.

---

## Search

Graph Memory uses hybrid search combining three strategies:

1. **BM25 keyword search** — exact and partial term matching with TF-IDF scoring
2. **Vector cosine similarity** — semantic similarity using embedding vectors
3. **BFS graph expansion** — after finding initial matches, traverses graph edges to discover related nodes

Results are fused using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and filtered by `minScore` threshold.

### Search parameters

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | (required) | Search query text |
| `maxResults` | `5` | Maximum results to return |
| `minScore` | `0.5` | Minimum relevance threshold (0–1) |
| `bfsDepth` | `1` | BFS expansion depth (0 = no expansion) |
| `bfsDecay` | `0.5` | Score decay per BFS hop |
| `searchMode` | `"hybrid"` | `"hybrid"`, `"keyword"`, or `"vector"` |

---

## MCP Tools Reference

70 tools organized by graph type. All tools use the `graph_verb_noun` naming convention.

### Context (1 tool)

#### `get_context`

Returns current project and workspace context. No parameters. Always call this at the start of a session.

Returns: `{ projectId, workspaceId?, workspaceProjects?, hasWorkspace }`

---

### Documentation Tools (10 tools)

#### `docs_list_files`

Lists all indexed markdown files.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `filter` | No | — | Substring filter on file path or title |
| `limit` | No | — | Maximum number of results |

Returns: `[{ fileId, title, chunks }]`

#### `docs_get_toc`

Returns table of contents for a documentation file.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `fileId` | Yes | File ID (e.g. `"docs/auth.md"`) |

Returns: `[{ id, title, level }]`

#### `docs_search`

Hybrid search across documentation sections with BFS graph expansion.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | Yes | — | Search query (natural language) |
| `topK` | No | 5 | Seed results for BFS expansion |
| `bfsDepth` | No | 1 | BFS expansion hops |
| `maxResults` | No | 5 | Maximum results returned |
| `minScore` | No | 0.5 | Minimum relevance score (0–1) |
| `bfsDecay` | No | 0.8 | Score decay per BFS hop |
| `searchMode` | No | `hybrid` | `hybrid`, `vector`, or `keyword` |

Returns: `[{ id, fileId, title, content, level, score }]`

#### `docs_get_node`

Retrieves full content of a specific documentation section.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `nodeId` | Yes | Node ID (e.g. `"docs/auth.md::JWT Tokens"`) |

Returns: `{ id, fileId, title, content, level, mtime }`

#### `docs_search_files`

File-level semantic search on documentation files.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | Yes | — | Search query |
| `limit` | No | 10 | Maximum results |
| `minScore` | No | 0.3 | Minimum relevance score |

Returns: `[{ fileId, title, chunks, score }]`

#### `docs_list_snippets`

Lists fenced code blocks extracted from documentation.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `fileId` | No | — | Filter by documentation file |
| `language` | No | — | Filter by language (e.g. `"typescript"`) |
| `filter` | No | — | Substring match on code content |
| `limit` | No | 20 | Maximum results |

Returns: `[{ id, fileId, language, symbols, preview }]`

#### `docs_search_snippets`

Semantic search over code blocks extracted from documentation.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | Yes | — | Search query |
| `topK` | No | 10 | Maximum results |
| `minScore` | No | 0.3 | Minimum relevance score |
| `language` | No | — | Filter by language |

Returns: `[{ id, fileId, language, symbols, content, score }]`

#### `docs_find_examples`

Finds code blocks in documentation that mention a specific symbol.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `symbol` | Yes | — | Symbol name (e.g. `"UserService"`) |
| `limit` | No | 20 | Maximum results |

Returns: `[{ id, fileId, language, symbols, content, parentId, parentTitle }]`

#### `docs_explain_symbol`

Finds a code example for a symbol along with its surrounding prose explanation.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `symbol` | Yes | — | Symbol name to look up |
| `limit` | No | 10 | Maximum results |

Returns: `[{ codeBlock, explanation, fileId }]`

#### `docs_cross_references`

Returns complete picture for a symbol: source code definition, documentation mentions, and code examples. Use this instead of making separate calls to `code_search`, `docs_search`, and `docs_find_examples`.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `symbol` | Yes | Symbol name (e.g. `"hashPassword"`) |

Returns: `{ definitions: [...], documentation: [...], examples: [...] }`

---

### Code Tools (5 tools)

#### `code_list_files`

Lists all indexed source code files.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `filter` | No | — | Substring filter on file path |
| `limit` | No | — | Maximum results |

Returns: `[{ fileId, symbolCount }]`

#### `code_get_file_symbols`

Lists all symbols in a source file.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `fileId` | Yes | File ID (e.g. `"src/auth.ts"`) |

Returns: `[{ id, kind, name, signature, startLine, endLine, isExported }]`

#### `code_search`

Hybrid search over code symbols with BFS graph expansion.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | Yes | — | Search query |
| `topK` | No | 5 | Seed results for BFS |
| `bfsDepth` | No | 1 | BFS expansion hops |
| `maxResults` | No | 5 | Maximum results |
| `minScore` | No | 0.3 | Minimum relevance score |
| `bfsDecay` | No | 0.8 | Score decay per hop |
| `searchMode` | No | `hybrid` | `hybrid`, `vector`, or `keyword` |
| `includeBody` | No | `false` | Include the full source body in results |

Returns: `[{ id, fileId, kind, name, signature, docComment, startLine, endLine, score, body? }]`

#### `code_get_symbol`

Retrieves full source body of a specific symbol.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `nodeId` | Yes | Symbol node ID (e.g. `"src/auth.ts::hashPassword"`) |

Returns: `{ id, fileId, kind, name, signature, docComment, body, startLine, endLine, isExported, crossLinks? }`

#### `code_search_files`

File-level semantic search over source files.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | Yes | — | Search query |
| `limit` | No | 10 | Maximum results |
| `minScore` | No | 0.3 | Minimum relevance score |

Returns: `[{ fileId, symbolCount, score }]`

---

### Knowledge Tools (12 tools)

#### `notes_create` (mutation)

Creates a new note with automatic slug ID generation.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `title` | Yes | Note title |
| `content` | Yes | Note content (markdown) |
| `tags` | No | Array of tags |

Returns: `{ noteId }`

#### `notes_update` (mutation)

Partially updates a note.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `noteId` | Yes | Note ID to update |
| `title` | No | New title |
| `content` | No | New content |
| `tags` | No | New tags (replaces existing) |

Returns: `{ noteId, updated }`

#### `notes_delete` (mutation)

Deletes a note and all its relations.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `noteId` | Yes | Note ID to delete |

Returns: `{ noteId, deleted }`

#### `notes_get`

Fetches a note by ID.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `noteId` | Yes | Note ID |

Returns: `{ id, title, content, tags, createdAt, updatedAt }`

#### `notes_list`

Lists notes with optional filters.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `filter` | No | — | Substring match on title |
| `tag` | No | — | Filter by tag |
| `limit` | No | 20 | Maximum results |

Returns: `[{ id, title, tags, updatedAt }]`

#### `notes_search`

Hybrid search over notes with BFS graph expansion.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | Yes | — | Search query |
| `topK` | No | 5 | Seed results for BFS |
| `bfsDepth` | No | 1 | BFS expansion hops |
| `maxResults` | No | 5 | Maximum results |
| `minScore` | No | 0.5 | Minimum relevance score |
| `bfsDecay` | No | 0.8 | Score decay per hop |
| `searchMode` | No | `hybrid` | `hybrid`, `vector`, or `keyword` |

Returns: `[{ id, title, content, tags, score }]`

#### `notes_create_link` (mutation)

Creates a typed relation from a note to another note or to a node in another graph.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `fromId` | Yes | Source note ID |
| `toId` | Yes | Target note ID or external node ID |
| `kind` | Yes | Relation type (e.g. `"references"`, `"contradicts"`, `"extends"`) |
| `targetGraph` | No | Target graph: `"docs"`, `"code"`, `"files"`, `"tasks"`, `"skills"` |
| `projectId` | No | Target project ID (for cross-project links in workspaces) |

Returns: `{ fromId, toId, kind, targetGraph?, created }`

#### `notes_delete_link` (mutation)

Deletes a relation and cleans up orphaned proxy nodes.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `fromId` | Yes | Source note ID |
| `toId` | Yes | Target note ID or external node ID |
| `targetGraph` | No | Target graph (if cross-graph link) |
| `projectId` | No | Target project ID |

Returns: `{ fromId, toId, deleted }`

#### `notes_list_links`

Lists all relations for a note (incoming and outgoing).

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `noteId` | Yes | Note ID |

Returns: `[{ fromId, toId, kind, targetGraph? }]`

#### `notes_find_linked`

Reverse lookup: finds all notes that link to a specific node in another graph.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `targetId` | Yes | Target node ID |
| `targetGraph` | Yes | Which graph: `"docs"`, `"code"`, `"files"`, `"tasks"`, `"skills"` |
| `kind` | No | Filter by relation kind |
| `projectId` | No | Target project ID |

Returns: `[{ noteId, title, kind, tags }]`

#### `notes_add_attachment` (mutation)

Attaches a file to a note. Max 10 MB per file, 20 attachments per note.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `noteId` | Yes | Note ID |
| `filePath` | Yes | Absolute path to the file |

Returns: `{ filename, mimeType, size, addedAt }`

#### `notes_remove_attachment` (mutation)

Removes a file attachment from a note.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `noteId` | Yes | Note ID |
| `filename` | Yes | Filename to remove |

Returns: `{ deleted: filename }`

---

### Task Tools (13 tools)

#### `tasks_create` (mutation)

Creates a new task.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `title` | Yes | — | Task title |
| `description` | Yes | — | Task description (markdown) |
| `priority` | Yes | — | `critical`, `high`, `medium`, `low` |
| `status` | No | `backlog` | `backlog`, `todo`, `in_progress`, `review`, `done`, `cancelled` |
| `tags` | No | — | Array of tags |
| `dueDate` | No | — | Due date (Unix ms) |
| `estimate` | No | — | Estimated effort in hours |
| `assignee` | No | — | Assignee name |

Returns: `{ taskId }`

#### `tasks_update` (mutation)

Partially updates a task.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `taskId` | Yes | Task ID |
| (any field) | No | Any `tasks_create` field |
| `expectedVersion` | No | Optimistic locking — fails if task was updated since |

Returns: `{ taskId, updated }`

#### `tasks_delete` (mutation)

Deletes a task and all its relations.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `taskId` | Yes | Task ID |

Returns: `{ taskId, deleted }`

#### `tasks_get`

Fetches a task with all relations and cross-graph links.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `taskId` | Yes | Task ID |

Returns: `{ id, title, description, status, priority, tags, dueDate, estimate, assignee, completedAt, createdAt, updatedAt, version, attachments, subtasks, blockedBy, blocks, related, crossLinks? }`

#### `tasks_list`

Lists tasks with optional filters, sorted by priority then due date.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `status` | No | — | Filter by status |
| `priority` | No | — | Filter by priority |
| `tag` | No | — | Filter by tag |
| `filter` | No | — | Substring match on title |
| `assignee` | No | — | Filter by assignee |
| `limit` | No | 50 | Maximum results |

Returns: `[{ id, title, description, status, priority, tags, dueDate, estimate, assignee, completedAt, version }]` (descriptions truncated to 500 chars)

#### `tasks_search`

Hybrid search over tasks.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | Yes | — | Search query |
| `topK` | No | 5 | Seed results for BFS |
| `bfsDepth` | No | 1 | BFS expansion hops |
| `maxResults` | No | 5 | Maximum results |
| `minScore` | No | 0.5 | Minimum relevance score |
| `bfsDecay` | No | 0.8 | Score decay per hop |
| `searchMode` | No | `hybrid` | `hybrid`, `vector`, or `keyword` |

Returns: `[{ id, title, description, status, priority, tags, score }]`

#### `tasks_move` (mutation)

Changes a task's status. Automatically sets `completedAt` on `done`/`cancelled`, clears it on other statuses. Always use this instead of `tasks_update` for status changes.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `taskId` | Yes | Task ID |
| `status` | Yes | New status |
| `expectedVersion` | No | Optimistic locking |

Returns: `{ taskId, status, completedAt }`

#### `tasks_link` (mutation)

Creates a relation between two tasks.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `fromId` | Yes | Source task ID |
| `toId` | Yes | Target task ID |
| `kind` | Yes | `subtask_of`, `blocks`, or `related_to` |

Returns: `{ fromId, toId, kind, created }`

#### `tasks_create_link` (mutation)

Creates a cross-graph link from a task.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `taskId` | Yes | Source task ID |
| `targetId` | Yes | Target node ID |
| `targetGraph` | Yes | `"docs"`, `"code"`, `"files"`, `"knowledge"`, `"skills"` |
| `kind` | Yes | Relation type |
| `projectId` | No | Target project ID |

Returns: `{ taskId, targetId, targetGraph, kind, created }`

#### `tasks_delete_link` (mutation)

Deletes a cross-graph link from a task.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `taskId` | Yes | Task ID |
| `targetId` | Yes | Target node ID |
| `targetGraph` | Yes | Target graph |
| `projectId` | No | Target project ID |

Returns: `{ taskId, targetId, deleted }`

#### `tasks_find_linked`

Reverse lookup: finds all tasks linked to a node in another graph.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `targetId` | Yes | Target node ID |
| `targetGraph` | Yes | Which graph |
| `kind` | No | Filter by relation kind |
| `projectId` | No | Target project ID |

Returns: `[{ taskId, title, kind, status, priority, tags }]`

#### `tasks_add_attachment` (mutation)

Attaches a file to a task. Max 10 MB per file, 20 attachments per task.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `taskId` | Yes | Task ID |
| `filePath` | Yes | Absolute path to the file |

Returns: `{ filename, mimeType, size, addedAt }`

#### `tasks_remove_attachment` (mutation)

Removes a file attachment from a task.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `taskId` | Yes | Task ID |
| `filename` | Yes | Filename to remove |

Returns: `{ deleted: filename }`

---

### Skill Tools (14 tools)

#### `skills_create` (mutation)

Creates a new skill (reusable procedure or recipe).

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `title` | Yes | — | Skill title |
| `description` | Yes | — | What this skill does |
| `steps` | No | — | Array of step strings (ordered procedure) |
| `triggers` | No | — | Array of trigger phrases that activate this skill |
| `inputHints` | No | — | Array of expected inputs or prerequisites |
| `filePatterns` | No | — | Glob patterns for relevant files (e.g. `["src/**/*.ts"]`) |
| `tags` | No | — | Array of tags |
| `source` | No | `user` | `"user"` or `"learned"` |
| `confidence` | No | 1.0 | Confidence score (0–1) |

Returns: `{ skillId }`

#### `skills_update` (mutation)

Partially updates a skill.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `skillId` | Yes | Skill ID |
| (any field) | No | Any `skills_create` field |

Returns: `{ skillId, updated }`

#### `skills_delete` (mutation)

Deletes a skill and all its relations.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `skillId` | Yes | Skill ID |

Returns: `{ skillId, deleted }`

#### `skills_get`

Fetches a skill with all relations and metadata.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `skillId` | Yes | Skill ID |

Returns: `{ id, title, description, steps, triggers, inputHints, filePatterns, source, confidence, tags, usageCount, lastUsedAt, createdAt, updatedAt, dependsOn, dependedBy, related, variants, crossLinks? }`

#### `skills_list`

Lists skills with optional filters.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `source` | No | — | Filter by source |
| `tag` | No | — | Filter by tag |
| `filter` | No | — | Substring match on title |
| `limit` | No | 50 | Maximum results |

Returns: `[{ id, title, source, tags, usageCount, lastUsedAt }]`

#### `skills_search`

Hybrid search over skills.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | Yes | — | Search query |
| `topK` | No | 5 | Seed results for BFS |
| `bfsDepth` | No | 1 | BFS expansion hops |
| `maxResults` | No | 5 | Maximum results |
| `minScore` | No | 0.5 | Minimum relevance score |
| `bfsDecay` | No | 0.8 | Score decay per hop |
| `searchMode` | No | `hybrid` | `hybrid`, `vector`, or `keyword` |

Returns: `[{ id, title, description, source, confidence, usageCount, tags, score }]`

#### `skills_recall`

Broad skill recall with lower relevance threshold. Use at the start of non-trivial tasks to catch tangentially relevant procedures.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `context` | Yes | — | Description of the current task or situation |
| `topK` | No | 5 | Maximum results |
| `minScore` | No | 0.3 | Minimum relevance score (lower than `skills_search`) |

Returns: `[{ id, title, description, steps, triggers, source, tags, score, usageCount }]` (includes full steps)

#### `skills_bump_usage` (mutation)

Increments a skill's usage counter and updates its last-used timestamp. Call this after a skill helped with a task.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `skillId` | Yes | Skill ID |

Returns: `{ skillId, usageCount, lastUsedAt }`

#### `skills_link` (mutation)

Creates a relation between two skills.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `fromId` | Yes | Source skill ID |
| `toId` | Yes | Target skill ID |
| `kind` | Yes | `depends_on`, `related_to`, or `variant_of` |

Returns: `{ fromId, toId, kind, created }`

#### `skills_create_link` (mutation)

Creates a cross-graph link from a skill.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `skillId` | Yes | Source skill ID |
| `targetId` | Yes | Target node ID |
| `targetGraph` | Yes | `"docs"`, `"code"`, `"files"`, `"knowledge"`, `"tasks"` |
| `kind` | Yes | Relation type |
| `projectId` | No | Target project ID |

Returns: `{ skillId, targetId, targetGraph, kind, created }`

#### `skills_delete_link` (mutation)

Deletes a cross-graph link from a skill.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `skillId` | Yes | Skill ID |
| `targetId` | Yes | Target node ID |
| `targetGraph` | Yes | Target graph |
| `projectId` | No | Target project ID |

Returns: `{ skillId, targetId, deleted }`

#### `skills_find_linked`

Reverse lookup: finds all skills linked to a node in another graph.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `targetId` | Yes | Target node ID |
| `targetGraph` | Yes | Which graph |
| `kind` | No | Filter by relation kind |
| `projectId` | No | Target project ID |

Returns: `[{ skillId, title, kind, source, tags }]`

#### `skills_add_attachment` (mutation)

Attaches a file to a skill. Max 10 MB per file, 20 attachments per skill.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `skillId` | Yes | Skill ID |
| `filePath` | Yes | Absolute path to the file |

Returns: `{ filename, mimeType, size, addedAt }`

#### `skills_remove_attachment` (mutation)

Removes a file attachment from a skill.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `skillId` | Yes | Skill ID |
| `filename` | Yes | Filename to remove |

Returns: `{ deleted: filename }`

---

### File Index Tools (3 tools)

#### `files_list`

Lists all project files and directories with optional filters.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `directory` | No | — | Filter by parent directory (e.g. `"src/lib"`) |
| `extension` | No | — | Filter by extension (e.g. `".ts"`) |
| `language` | No | — | Filter by language (e.g. `"typescript"`) |
| `filter` | No | — | Substring match on file path |
| `limit` | No | 50 | Maximum results |

Returns: `[{ filePath, kind, fileName, extension, language, mimeType, size, fileCount }]`

#### `files_search`

Semantic search over all project files by path and name.

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | Yes | — | Search query |
| `topK` | No | 10 | Maximum results |
| `minScore` | No | 0.3 | Minimum relevance score |

Returns: `[{ filePath, fileName, extension, language, size, score }]`

#### `files_get_info`

Returns full metadata for a specific file or directory.

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `filePath` | Yes | File path relative to project root (e.g. `"src/lib/embedder.ts"`) |

Returns: `{ filePath, kind, fileName, directory, extension, language, mimeType, size, fileCount, mtime }`

---

## Best Practices for AI Assistants

1. **Start with `get_context`** — understand which project and workspace you're in
2. **Search before creating** — avoid duplicate notes, tasks, and skills
3. **Use search tools, not list tools** — hybrid search is much more effective than manual browsing
4. **Recall skills for complex tasks** — `skills_recall` uses a lower threshold to catch relevant procedures
5. **Use cross-graph links** — connect notes to code, tasks to files, skills to docs
6. **Use `docs_cross_references`** — get source + docs + examples for a symbol in one call
7. **Use `tasks_move` for status changes** — it manages `completedAt` automatically
8. **Manage context budget** — lower `maxResults`, raise `minScore`, use TOC before fetching full content
9. **Link everything** — the more connections, the better BFS graph expansion works

---

## Authentication

### Password login (UI)

Users log in at `/ui/auth/signin`. Passwords are hashed with scrypt (N=65536). Sessions use JWT cookies (httpOnly, SameSite=Lax).

### API keys (programmatic)

```yaml
server:
  auth:
    apiKeys:
      - name: ci-bot
        key: "gm_your_api_key_here"
        access: readonly
```

Use via `Authorization: Bearer gm_your_api_key_here` header.

### OAuth 2.0

Two flows supported:

- **Client Credentials** — for server-to-server (Claude.ai, scripts)
- **Authorization Code + PKCE (S256)** — for browser-based clients

Discovery: `GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`

Endpoints: `/api/oauth/authorize`, `/api/oauth/token`, `/api/oauth/userinfo`, `/api/oauth/introspect`, `/api/oauth/revoke`, `/api/oauth/end-session`

---

## Security

- **Rate limiting**: 600 req/min global, 120 req/min search, 10 req/min auth
- **CSRF protection**: SameSite cookies + API keys for non-browser clients
- **XSS prevention**: httpOnly cookies, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
- **Path traversal**: multi-level filename validation
- **Session timeout**: configurable, default 60 minutes
- **CORS**: credentials enabled, origins configurable
- **Headers**: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options: DENY

---

## File Mirror

Notes, tasks, and skills are mirrored to markdown files:

```
your-project/
  .notes/
    my-note/
      index.md          # note content with YAML frontmatter
      attachments/      # uploaded files
  .tasks/
    fix-bug/
      index.md
  .skills/
    deploy-to-prod/
      index.md
```

Edit these files in your IDE — changes are automatically detected and synced back to the graph (reverse import).

---

## REST API

All endpoints under `/api/projects/:projectId/`.

| Endpoint | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `GET /api/projects/:id/docs/search` | Search docs |
| `GET /api/projects/:id/code/search` | Search code |
| `GET /api/projects/:id/knowledge` | List notes |
| `POST /api/projects/:id/knowledge` | Create note |
| `GET /api/projects/:id/tasks` | List tasks |
| `POST /api/projects/:id/tasks` | Create task |
| `GET /api/projects/:id/skills` | List skills |
| `POST /api/projects/:id/skills` | Create skill |
| `GET /api/projects/:id/files` | List files |
| `POST /api/embed` | Compute embeddings via the server's model |

WebSocket at `/ws` for real-time mutation events.

---

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@graphmemory/server
- Docker: ghcr.io/graph-memory/graphmemory-server
- Website: https://graphmemory.dev
- Changelog: https://graphmemory.dev/changelog
